title: crocus hybernus subsp. asturicus
intro: Crocus hybernus subsp. asturicus, a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the autumn-flowering Crocus, is a type of bulbous perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crocus hybernus subsp. asturicus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide protection against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
